headline: Postpartum Care Coverage Ends at 42 Days Leaving Mothers Unsupported
context: Government postnatal care protocol provides only 3 home visits within 42 days with no support thereafter.

cards.big: Postnatal care protocol provides only 3 home visits ending at 42 days postpartum
cards.sub: Complications including depression infection and breastfeeding difficulties peak after day 42

cards.big: 28% of postpartum hospital readmissions occur between days 43 and 90 after delivery
cards.sub: Care coverage gap coincides with a period of significant maternal health vulnerability

cards.big: Community nurses manage average caseload of 180 postnatal mothers simultaneously
cards.sub: High caseloads mean each home visit lasts only 15-20 minutes insufficient for thorough assessment

cards.big: Netherlands provides 8 days of postnatal home care plus 6 months of community nurse support
cards.sub: Extended coverage model reduces maternal complications and improves infant health outcomes

cards.big: Ending maternal support at 42 days when complications continue is arbitrary not evidence-based
Should postnatal care coverage be extended to 6 months with scheduled check-ups throughout
